findings.message: get_stock now reads `reserved_count` from the inventory response, but the consumer Pact interaction in tests/functional/test_consumer_inventory.py (line 19) only declares `item_id` and `in_stock`. The consumer now relies on a field that the contract does not pin, so provider verification cannot guarantee the provider returns `reserved_count`, and the consumer Pact mock (which omits the field) will make `int(data['reserved_count'])` raise KeyError. The pact interaction must be extended to declare `reserved_count` before the client depends on it.
findings.suggestion: Add `reserved_count` to the consumer pact body in test_consumer_inventory.py (e.g. `.with_body({"item_id": match.int(1), "in_stock": match.int(5), "reserved_count": match.int(0)})`) and regenerate/publish the pact so the provider is verified against the new contracted field.
